prompt:
A InstituteOps Offline Learning, Inventory, and Group-Buy Administration system with main features including student lifecycle management, instructor-grade entry with auditable credit calculations, ingredient inventory and procurement controls, and on-site group-buy campaigns for program materials and bundled supplies. Staff and students use a browser-based interface rendered with Thymeleaf templates, optimized for quick keyboard data entry and clear status feedback without relying on any internet connection. Roles include System Administrator, Registrar/Finance Clerk, Instructor, Inventory Manager, Procurement Approver, Store Manager, and Student. The student area shows masked contact details by default and a timeline of enrollment, payments recorded as cash/check/other offline methods, class/session attendance, optional instructor comments, and homework attachments (PDF/JPG up to 10 MB each, virus scanning excluded; file type and checksum validation only). Instructors enter grades into a traceable ledger that immediately shows earned credits and GPA impact using the currently active ruleset, while also allowing “what changed” delta comparisons when a score is edited. The store area supports SPU/SKU catalog with specs, tiered pricing (for example, $29.00 each or 3+ at $25.00), per-student purchase limits (default 5 units), campaign windows with a visible countdown, and an order cutoff time (for example, 9:00 PM). Students can join or start a group, see formation rules (default: 10 participants within 72 hours), and receive on-screen confirmation when inventory is locked for their pending order; if a campaign fails, the UI automatically marks orders as void with no payment capture.
The backend uses Spring Boot to implement server-side MVC pages and REST-style APIs for decoupled UI actions (search, imports, recalculation, inventory transactions) while persisting all data locally in MySQL. Grade Entry & Credit Calculation is stored as an append-only ledger with operator logging, timestamps, and a rule-version audit trail; recalculation is deterministic and repeatable, and any recompute must store the previous result, the new result, and the delta, with an immutable reason code required for manual overrides. Inventory and procurement maintain multi-unit conversions (e.g., 1 case = 24 cans; 1 lb = 16 oz), batch and shelf-life tracking with FIFO picking, stock counts with variance thresholds (flag variances over 2% or over $50.00), and mandatory loss reasons (spoilage, damage, theft, prep waste). Procurement runs as a closed loop from request to approval to receiving, retains supplier profiles and price fluctuation history, and generates low-stock and near-expiry alerts (default: low stock at 7-day average usage; near-expiry at 10 days) plus replenishment recommendations based on trailing 30-day consumption. Data governance includes bulk import/export (CSV), consistency checks, duplicate detection (exact and fuzzy matches on name + DOB), change history, soft delete, and a recycle bin with 30-day retention; optional scheduled sync APIs are LAN-only and disabled by default. Access and compliance controls enforce local username/password login (bcrypt), least-privilege role permissions, token/key-based API auth for internal clients, encryption-at-rest settings for sensitive fields (AES-256 for contact and payment references), and comprehensive operation and data-access logs. The offline Collaborative Filtering recommender runs on locally stored events (views, orders, enrollments), supports switchable UserCF/ItemCF with cosine/Jaccard/adjusted cosine, time decay (half-life 14 days), popularity penalty, incremental updates on new events, and model version management so admins can roll back recommendations if a configuration change degrades results.
